The number 91 might be noted as \( 7 \times 13 \). To find the sum of its positive divisors. Let's first write all the combinations of the products of 7 and 13. Hence, the divisors of 91 are \( 1, 7, 13, \) and \( 91 \). So, if we were to sum them, it would be \{ 1 \} + \{ 7 \} + \{ 13 \} + \{ 91 \}. Or, we can use the equation for finding the sum of the divisors of a number based on its prime factorization, which is given by \( ( \frac{p^{a+1} - 1}{p-1} )( \frac{q^{b+1} - 1}{q-1} ) \ldots \). For 91, it is \( ( \frac{7^2 - 1}{7-1} )( \frac{13^1 - 1}{13-1} ) = ( \frac{49 - 1}{6} )( \frac{13 - 1}{12} ) = ( \frac{48}{6} )( \frac{12}{12} ) = 8 \times 1 = 8 \text{ (which is the sum of divisors excluding 91)} + 91 = 100. ]
